description_long: This is a classic turntable record player with a rectangular base featuring a metallic gold finish on the top surface and black sides. The circular turntable platter is centrally located and bordered by a textured, silver edge, likely for grip and visual contrast. A prominent tonearm, finished in gold, extends from the right side and includes a visible counterweight and headshell, resting in its cradle. The front panel displays several control buttons and knobs, including a start/stop button and speed selector, all integrated into the gold surface. Four sturdy, black feet elevate the device, providing stability and vibration isolation. The overall design is sleek and functional, with a retro-modern aesthetic suitable for urban or home environments.
description: A gold and black turntable record player with a central platter, gold tonearm, and front-facing control buttons. The design is sleek, retro, and functional, with visible mechanical details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular platter, gold tonearm to the right, and control buttons on the front panel.
description_view_1: Right-side view highlighting the full length of the gold tonearm and the platter's edge, with control buttons partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view with the tonearm on the left, platter in the center, and minimal visible controls.
description_view_3: Left-side view focusing on the tonearm's base and the platter, with the gold finish and feet visible.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, centrally located, and circular with a textured silver edge for grip. It occupies a significant portion of the top surface, is black in color, and appears to have a smooth, slightly glossy finish. No grooves or patterns are visible on the main surface, but the edge is distinctly ribbed.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, gold in color, and extends from the right side of the base. It is straight with a visible counterweight at the rear and a headshell at the front. The tonearm is resting in its cradle, parallel to the platter, and appears to be made of metal with a glossy finish.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its location is implied at the end of the tonearm's headshell. It would be small and likely metallic or ceramic, attached as a separate component.
attribute_control_knobs: Several control knobs and buttons are present on the front and top right of the device. They are round or rectangular, made of plastic or metal, and colored gold or black to match the device. Some buttons have visible labels or markings, such as a start/stop button and speed selector.
